api/v3/handlers/llmcost.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for the LLM cost price catalog: listing system and manual prices, getting a price by ID, and managing per-namespace manual overrides (create/list/delete). Bridges llmcost.Service to HTTP via the httptransport pipeline.

patterns.name: Decimal-as-string via decimalFromString
patterns.description: All per-token pricing amounts are api strings parsed with decimalFromString (alpacadecimal.NewFromString wrapped in models.NewGenericValidationError). Outbound, decimals are serialized with .String(). Never accept a decimal string without going through decimalFromString.
patterns.example: inputPerToken, err := decimalFromString(p.InputPerToken)

patterns.name: Provider display-name mapping with title-case fallback
patterns.description: providerDisplayNames (static map in convert.go) maps known provider IDs to display names; formatProviderName falls back to splitting on hyphen/underscore and capitalizing. Never hardcode provider display names in handler files.
patterns.example: Provider: api.LLMCostProvider{Id: providerID, Name: formatProviderName(providerID)}

patterns.name: Source enum one-directional mapping
patterns.description: Internal PriceSourceManual maps to api.LLMCostPriceSourceManual; everything else maps to api.LLMCostPriceSourceSystem. Reads only ever expose system/manual.
patterns.example: source := api.LLMCostPriceSourceSystem; if p.Source == llmcost.PriceSourceManual { source = api.LLMCostPriceSourceManual }

patterns.name: Sort-field allowlist validation
patterns.description: list_prices validates parsed sort fields against validPriceSortField before calling the service, returning apierrors.NewBadRequestError with the full valid-field list on failure.
patterns.example: if !validPriceSortField(sort.Field) { return req, apierrors.NewBadRequestError(ctx, ..., {Field: "sort", Reason: "...must be one of: id, provider.id, model.id, effective_from, effective_to"}) }

patterns.name: Nil-guarded optional pricing fields
patterns.description: Optional fields (CacheReadPerToken, CacheWritePerToken, ReasoningPerToken) are *alpacadecimal.Decimal in domain and *string in API; use lo.ToPtr(d.String()) only when non-nil.
patterns.example: if p.CacheReadPerToken != nil { out.CacheReadPerToken = lo.ToPtr(p.CacheReadPerToken.String()) }

patterns.name: Filter parsing via filters.FromAPIFilterString
patterns.description: All string filters (provider, model_id, model_name, currency, and source) are parsed with filters.FromAPIFilterString, each wrapped in its own bad-request error path. Source is a string filter, not an enum filter.
patterns.example: provider, err := filters.FromAPIFilterString(params.Filter.Provider)

patterns.name: Asymmetric list capabilities (prices vs overrides)
patterns.description: list_prices supports sort; list_overrides does not. Overrides are per-namespace and small — do not add sort to list_overrides without first updating the service layer.
patterns.example: // list_overrides.go has no Sort params block — intentionally omitted

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (5 methods) and handler struct with resolveNamespace, llmcost.Service, options; New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only one domain service (llmcost.Service) is injected — no secondary services.

key_file_guides.role: All domain<->API conversions: domainPriceToAPI, domainPricingToAPI, apiPricingToDomain, apiCreateOverrideToDomain, decimalFromString, formatProviderName, validPriceSortField, providerDisplayNames.
key_file_guides.watch_for: decimalFromString returns a wrapped validation error — propagate it, never ignore. Adding a provider requires updating the static providerDisplayNames map.

key_file_guides.role: Pagination (default page 1 / size 20), sort with allowlist, and five filters (provider, model_id, model_name, currency, source) for the global catalog.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Sort field must pass validPriceSortField. Source filter is a string filter via FromAPIFilterString.

key_file_guides.role: Pagination and four filters (provider, model_id, model_name, currency) for per-namespace overrides; no sort.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No sort support — do not add without service-layer changes.

key_file_guides.role: Parses api.LLMCostOverrideCreate via request.ParseBody, resolves namespace, converts to domain via apiCreateOverrideToDomain, returns HTTP 201.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses bare httptransport.NewHandler (no path arg); validation errors from apiCreateOverrideToDomain must surface from the decoder.

key_file_guides.role: HandlerWithArgs[..., api.ULID] keyed on the path ULID; delete returns 204 via EmptyResponseEncoder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both set ID and Namespace on the domain input from the path arg + resolveNamespace.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding provider display names in handler logic instead of using formatProviderName.
Accepting decimal strings without decimalFromString — bypasses NewGenericValidationError wrapping.
Exposing internal PriceSource values directly instead of mapping through the system/manual enum.
Adding sort to list_overrides without updating the service layer.

decisions.decision: Provider display names in a static map with title-case fallback.
decisions.rationale: API consumers need human-readable names; a central map avoids scatter while the fallback handles unknown providers without code changes.

decisions.decision: list_prices supports sort while list_overrides does not.
decisions.rationale: Overrides are per-namespace and typically small; sort complexity would need service-layer changes not yet warranted.

code_examples.scenario: Converting a domain Price to API response with optional pricing fields

api/v3/handlers/meters/query.purpose: Shared query-parameter translation sub-package for the v3 meter query endpoint. Converts api.MeterQueryRequest into streaming.QueryParams, validates dimensions/filters against meter definitions, and resolves customer IDs — insulating the HTTP handler from all domain mapping.

patterns.name: ValidationIssue error factory in errors.go
patterns.description: Every user-facing validation error is a package-level models.ValidationIssue var built with NewValidationIssue + WithFieldString/WithCriticalSeverity/commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute. Public constructors attach runtime context via .WithAttr or .WithPathString.
patterns.example: var ErrInvalidWindowSize = models.NewValidationIssue(...); func NewInvalidWindowSizeError(d string) error { return ErrInvalidWindowSize.WithAttr("value", d) }

patterns.name: Whitelist-only filter operators (eq/in)
patterns.description: ExtractStringsFromQueryFilter and ExtractStringsFromQueryFilterMapItem reject every operator except eq and in by checking non-nil fields (Neq, Nin, Contains, Ncontains, And, Or, and Exists for MapItem) and returning NewUnsupportedFilterOperatorError. eq and in together is also rejected.
patterns.example: if f.Neq != nil || f.Nin != nil || f.Contains != nil { return nil, NewUnsupportedFilterOperatorError(fieldPath...) }

patterns.name: Reserved-dimension switch in BuildQueryParams
patterns.description: The dimensions loop switches on key: DimensionSubject, DimensionCustomerID, default (meter GroupBy lookup). A new reserved dimension must be added as a case here AND to IsReservedDimension in dimensions.go.
patterns.example: switch k { case DimensionSubject: ...; case DimensionCustomerID: ...; default: if _, ok := m.GroupBy[k]; !ok { return params, NewInvalidDimensionFilterError(k) } }

patterns.name: Deduplicated GroupBy via slices.Contains
patterns.description: Every path that appends to params.GroupBy (subject filter, customer filter, explicit GroupByDimensions) first calls slices.Contains to prevent duplicates.
patterns.example: if !slices.Contains(params.GroupBy, DimensionSubject) { params.GroupBy = append(params.GroupBy, DimensionSubject) }

patterns.name: CustomerResolverFunc dependency injection
patterns.description: Customer lookup is a CustomerResolverFunc type passed into BuildQueryParams, not a direct service reference — enabling noop injection in tests. NewCustomerResolver(svc) wires the real lookup with IncludeDeleted: true.
patterns.example: func BuildQueryParams(ctx, m meter.Meter, body api.MeterQueryRequest, resolveCustomers CustomerResolverFunc) (streaming.QueryParams, error)

patterns.name: ISO8601 <-> WindowSize lookup tables
patterns.description: Bidirectional conversion uses package-level maps iso8601ToWindowSize / windowSizeToISO8601. A new window size requires updating both maps and the ErrInvalidWindowSize message.
patterns.example: var iso8601ToWindowSize = map[string]meter.WindowSize{"PT1M": meter.WindowSizeMinute, "PT1H": ..., "P1D": ..., "P1M": ...}

patterns.name: Filter complexity depth cap
patterns.description: Non-reserved GroupBy dimension filters are validated with f.ValidateWithComplexity(maxGroupByFilterComplexityDepth) (currently 2). Raising the constant widens the nested-filter attack surface.
patterns.example: const maxGroupByFilterComplexityDepth = 2

key_file_guides.role: BuildQueryParams — the single entry point converting MeterQueryRequest to streaming.QueryParams with full validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Go map iteration order is non-deterministic; GroupBy slice order may vary — tests must not assert exact ordering.

key_file_guides.role: All typed validation errors: window size, time zone, group_by, dimension filter, unsupported operator, customer-not-found.
key_file_guides.watch_for: HTTP status is embedded via commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute — never return raw fmt.Errorf for user-facing errors; extend these sentinels.

key_file_guides.role: Stateless helpers: ISO8601<->WindowSize and the two Extract functions (eq/in only).
key_file_guides.watch_for: ExtractStringsFromQueryFilterMapItem also rejects f.Exists — the two Extract functions are NOT interchangeable.
NewCustomerResolver lists with IncludeDeleted: true and collects missing IDs via errors.Join — both behaviors are intentional.

key_file_guides.role: DimensionSubject/DimensionCustomerID constants, IsReservedDimension, IsSupportedGroupByDimension — single source of reserved dimension names.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsSupportedGroupByDimension checks meter.GroupBy — the meter must carry its GroupBy populated or custom dimensions are rejected.

anti_patterns: Returning raw fmt.Errorf for user-visible validation failures — use a models.ValidationIssue constructor from errors.go.
Adding a dimension key to BuildQueryParams without updating IsReservedDimension.
Appending to params.GroupBy without a slices.Contains dedup guard.
Injecting customer.Service directly into BuildQueryParams instead of passing a CustomerResolverFunc.
Supporting extra filter operators without updating BOTH Extract functions consistently.

decisions.decision: CustomerResolverFunc function type instead of an interface.
decisions.rationale: Minimal dependency surface — tests inject a one-line lambda; the handler passes NewCustomerResolver(svc) at wiring time.

decisions.decision: Strict whitelist of eq/in filter operators.
decisions.rationale: Bounds ClickHouse query complexity and index usage; rejecting Neq/Nin/Contains/And/Or at translation prevents unbounded scans reaching the analytics store.

decisions.decision: Package-level ValidationIssue sentinels with runtime WithAttr/WithPathString constructors.
decisions.rationale: Sentinels allow errors.Is checks; WithAttr attaches context without allocating a new type, consistent with the rest of the v3 stack.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new reserved dimension that resolves like customer_id

api/v3/handlers/plans/planaddons.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for the plan-addons sub-resource of the Plans API. Bridges generated api/v3 request/response types to planaddon.Service using the httptransport.HandlerWithArgs pattern for all CRUD operations.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs for all endpoints
patterns.description: Every operation uses httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs (never bare NewHandler) because all endpoints carry path parameters (planID and/or planAddonID).
patterns.example: CreatePlanAddonHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[CreatePlanAddonRequest, CreatePlanAddonResponse, CreatePlanAddonParams]

patterns.name: Type-alias triplet per operation file
patterns.description: Each file defines Request (aliasing the domain input struct, e.g. planaddon.CreatePlanAddonInput), Response (api.PlanAddon), Params, and a Handler alias before the factory method.
patterns.example: type ( CreatePlanAddonRequest = planaddon.CreatePlanAddonInput; CreatePlanAddonResponse = api.PlanAddon; CreatePlanAddonParams = string )

patterns.name: Namespace resolved first in every decoder
patterns.description: Every decoder calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) as its first domain step, returns early on error, and sets it on the NamespacedModel of the domain input.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return CreatePlanAddonRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder on every handler
patterns.description: Every options block passes httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) plus WithOperationName — never a custom encoder — so domain errors map uniformly to RFC 7807.
patterns.example: httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("create-plan-addon"), httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()))

patterns.name: ToAPIPlanAddon for all entity responses
patterns.description: Single-entity operations return ToAPIPlanAddon(*result) from convert.go; validation issues surface via ToAPIProductCatalogValidationErrors — never inlined in operation files.
patterns.example: return ToAPIPlanAddon(*a)

patterns.name: labels.ToMetadata / labels.FromMetadata round-tripping
patterns.description: Inbound labels convert via labels.ToMetadata(body.Labels) in the decoder; the reverse labels.FromMetadata(a.Metadata) runs inside ToAPIPlanAddon. ToMetadata errors must be returned from the decoder.
patterns.example: meta, err := labels.ToMetadata(body.Labels); if err != nil { return CreatePlanAddonRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: Nil-guard on mutating responses
patterns.description: Create and Update guard against a nil service result with an explicit error rather than dereferencing.
patterns.example: if a == nil { return CreatePlanAddonResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to create plan addon") }

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (5 methods) and handler struct with resolveNamespace, plan.Service (service), planaddon.Service (addonService), options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: plan.Service is currently unused in operation files; all mutations go through addonService. Do not remove service without checking the v3 server mount.

key_file_guides.role: ToAPIPlanAddon translation and ToAPIProductCatalogValidationErrors for models.ValidationIssues.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ValidationErrors come from a.AsProductCatalogPlanAddon().ValidationErrors() with the error discarded — a signature change there breaks convert.go silently.

key_file_guides.role: ListPlanAddons with pagination (default page 1 / size 20) and response.NewPagePaginationResponse wrapping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PlanIDs filter is always []string{params.PlanID} (single plan). Do not widen without changing the Params struct and domain input.

key_file_guides.role: Parses api.UpsertPlanAddonRequest; only sets req.Metadata when body.Labels is non-nil to preserve metadata on partial updates.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FromPlanPhase is always set as a pointer (&body.FromPlanPhase) — present even for empty string; intentional upsert semantics.

key_file_guides.role: Parses api.CreatePlanAddonRequest, resolves namespace + labels, calls CreatePlanAddon, returns HTTP 201.
key_file_guides.watch_for: labels.ToMetadata must run before building the request; its error must be returned from the decoder, not ignored.

key_file_guides.role: HandlerWithArgs keyed on a struct {PlanID, PlanAddonID}; delete returns 204 via EmptyResponseEncoder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Get builds GetPlanAddonInput from PlanAddonID only; delete passes both PlanID and ID.

anti_patterns: Using httptransport.NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs when the endpoint has path parameters.
Calling domain service methods from the response encoder closure — all service calls go in the operation closure.
Introducing context.Background() in decoder or operation closures — always propagate the ctx argument.
Adding business logic (validation, enrichment) in handler struct methods instead of decoder/operation closures.
Omitting apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder from the options block.

decisions.decision: planaddon.Service is injected separately from plan.Service.
decisions.rationale: Plan-addon operations are owned by the planaddon sub-domain; addon mutations go through their own service to keep domain boundaries clean.

decisions.decision: Request types alias domain input structs directly.
decisions.rationale: Reduces the translation surface — any domain input change surfaces immediately as a compile error in the decoder closure.

decisions.decision: Pagination defaults to page 1 / size 20 and validates before the domain call.
decisions.rationale: Centralizes page-validation as a 400 before invoking the domain layer, matching all v3 list handlers.

code_examples.scenario: A create operation with namespace + label conversion and HTTP 201
